title: Feature/batch norm
body:
Added a boolean `use_batch_norm` flag to the CNN and MLP feature extractors.  
- In **NatureCNN**, each convolutional block now conditionally inserts an `nn.BatchNorm2d` layer (before the ReLU) when `use_batch_norm=True`.  
- In **create_mlp**, an initial `nn.BatchNorm1d` may be applied to the inputs, and batch-norm modules can wrap each linear layer.  
All defaults remain unchanged (batch norm off), and existing behavior is fully preserved.

step_plan.summary: Add an optional use_batch_norm flag to NatureCNN and create_mlp to enable conditional BatchNorm2d/BatchNorm1d in CNN and MLP feature builders, and make CombinedExtractor accept/forward the flag so features_extractor_kwargs can enable batch norm for image subspaces. Defaults remain False to preserve existing behavior.

steps.operation: Add use_batch_norm parameter and conditional BatchNorm2d insertion
steps.reason: Expose an optional use_batch_norm: bool = False argument and, when True, insert nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels) after each Conv2d (before the ReLU) in the existing convolutional Sequential so image feature extraction can optionally use batch normalization without changing the default behavior.
steps.side_effects: Backward compatible by default (use_batch_norm defaults to False). If enabled, training dynamics may change (BatchNorm affects statistics and learning); saved checkpoints with different use_batch_norm settings are not directly compatible because enabling BN adds parameters (running_mean/var, weight, bias) that won't exist in state dicts from models built with use_batch_norm=False. Tests should be added to verify forward passes with use_batch_norm=True. No other modules need modification to call sites because the new parameter has a default.

steps.operation: Add use_batch_norm parameter to create_mlp and include BatchNorm1d among pre-linear modules when requested
steps.reason: Add use_batch_norm: bool = False to create_mlp so callers can request BatchNorm1d to be applied before linear layers. Implement by ensuring, when use_batch_norm is True, that BatchNorm1d is present in pre_linear_modules (e.g., set pre_linear_modules = [nn.BatchNorm1d] if None, or prepend/insert nn.BatchNorm1d into the existing list if not already present). This composes with existing pre_linear_modules/post_linear_modules behavior and preserves defaults.
steps.side_effects: Backward compatible by default. Enabling BN changes network architecture (adds BatchNorm1d modules) and will alter training dynamics. Saved models built with/without use_batch_norm will have different parameter sets; loading across configurations may fail unless handled. Ensure unit tests verify: create_mlp(..., use_batch_norm=True) constructs modules with BatchNorm1d and forward passes produce expected shapes. Callers that pass custom pre_linear_modules must still work; ensure the implementation appends/inserts without overwriting user-provided lists.

steps.operation: Accept and forward use_batch_norm in CombinedExtractor construction for image sub-extractors
steps.reason: CombinedExtractor composes per-key extractors (e.g., NatureCNN for image keys) and must accept/use the new use_batch_norm argument so that passing use_batch_norm via features_extractor_kwargs is accepted and forwarded to underlying NatureCNN instances. This avoids unexpected keyword errors when users pass use_batch_norm through BaseModel.features_extractor_kwargs.
steps.side_effects: Backward compatible by default. If use_batch_norm is forwarded and True, image sub-extractors created by CombinedExtractor will include BatchNorm2d layers which affects training and saved model parameter sets (see above). Update tests to ensure CombinedExtractor(..., use_batch_norm=True) instantiates sub-extractors correctly and that concatenated outputs keep the same shapes. Also ensure CombinedExtractor still accepts arbitrary features_extractor_kwargs without breaking other keys.
